description: Relative timing market: will Russia-Ukraine reach a ceasefire before the US and Iran do? At 14%, the market strongly favors a US-Iran deal coming first. Novel 50-50 resolution if neither ceasefire occurs by year-end.
question: Will Russia and Ukraine reach a ceasefire before the US and Iran reach a ceasefire?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if Russia-Ukraine ceasefire is officially announced before a US-Iran ceasefire. Resolves No if US-Iran comes first. Resolves 50-50 if neither occurs by December 31, 2026. Same-day announcements resolve by earlier ET timestamp.

triggers.description: Russia-Ukraine official ceasefire announced before US-Iran official ceasefire
triggers.source: Official announcements from Russia, Ukraine, US, and Iran

exclusions: Informal agreements, backchannel communications, de-escalation without announced agreement
Humanitarian pauses, tactical stand-downs, unilateral pauses
Partial ceasefires (energy infrastructure, Black Sea only) for Russia-Ukraine
Frameworks without explicit dated commitment to stop fighting
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: Novel 50-50 resolution if neither ceasefire occurs — unusual mechanism. Same-day tiebreaker uses ET timestamp, creating a potential edge case if announcements happen in different time zones near midnight. US-Iran ceasefire requires both governments to publicly confirm — backchannel de-escalation does not count.

catalyst_dates.event: March 31 Russia-Ukraine ceasefire deadline market resolves
April 30 Russia-Ukraine ceasefire deadline market resolves

resolution_source.name: Governments of Russia, Ukraine, United States, and Iran

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: No equivalent on Kalshi. Relative-timing geopolitical markets are unique to Polymarket.

related_markets.notes: Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by June 30 at 15% — if this resolves Yes, the relative market likely moves sharply
Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by April 30 at 3.9% — near-term signal
